name: Session 2026-04-07

messages.content:
I’ll audit this directly against the handoff again, with the current workspace as ground truth. I’ll first refresh the document and dirty diff, then patch any concrete miss instead of stopping at a checklist.

The document search confirms PR11 is the only unimplemented stage called out after the converged PR2-PR10 work: high-level earring attachment from a committed hole frame plus a testbed interaction path. I’m reading the exact API sketch and current surface-edit/deformer code now to see what can be implemented cleanly without inventing a UI framework the repo doesn’t have.

The current public surface-edit API only exposes the low-level `CommitDeformableRestSpaceHole`; there is no `BeginSurfaceEdit`, preview result, or accessory transform helper yet. The testbed has keyboard/mouse callbacks but no ImGui-style layer, so the clean PR11 path is likely a keyboard-driven testbed flow plus engine helpers that tests can exercise.
check the document carefully and see if you're missing for implementation or see if you can improve.

I’ll re-anchor this pass on the handoff text and current diff, then compare it against the implementation that exists now. I’ll patch only concrete gaps or simplifications I can justify from the document, and I’ll keep local `.cozter` session/upload artifacts out of the source work.

The status confirms the source work is limited to deformable asset/runtime/tests, with `.cozter` local artifacts still separate. The handoff search again singles out PR11 as the only future stage after the current PR2-PR10 work, so I’m reading that section and the API sketch in full before touching code.

The current source only exposes `CommitDeformableRestSpaceHole`; the PR11 names from the handoff (`BeginSurfaceEdit`, `PreviewHole`, `CommitHole`, `AttachAccessory`) are absent. I’m checking the runtime and testbed surfaces now to see the smallest clean API addition that matches existing architecture rather than creating a parallel framework.
if you have no more things to fix, commit and push to main

I’ll do one final status and verification pass before publishing. If the source tree still matches the converged state, I’ll commit the source/test changes only and push `main`, leaving local `.cozter` artifacts out unless they’re explicitly requested.

`main` already has 58 local commits ahead of `origin/main`; the current dirty set is the deformable source/tests plus `.cozter` local files. I’m going to verify the source diff and checks before creating the final commit on top of those local commits.

The formatting diff check is clean. The pending source commit is a focused deformable runtime/assets/test update; the `.cozter` session/upload artifacts are the only non-source dirt, so I’ll keep them unstaged.

The publish skill normally opens a PR, but your instruction is explicitly to push `main`; I’m following that repo memory and skipping the PR step. Next I’m rerunning the standard-sensitive checks and the Linux multi-config verification before staging.

The standard check caught two source files with line-ending/EOF shape drift. I’m going to correct those mechanically before running the build and test matrix.

I fixed the EOF shape on the two files the standard check flagged. The source diff is now clean for whitespace, CRLF, tabs, and final separator/newline rules; I’m building the three Linux configs next.

`dbg` builds cleanly; Ninja repeated the known “premature end of file; recovering” warning, but the build finished successfully. I’m continuing with `fin` and `opt`.

All three configs build successfully, again with only the known Ninja recovery warning. I’m running the explicit `ctest -C dbg/fin/opt` matrix now.

The full ctest matrix passed in all three configs, 7/7 each. I’m doing the isolated `resource_cooker` smokes against `impl/assets` and `Testbed/assets` next, since that has been the Linux convergence anchor for this work.

The isolated resource cooker smoke passed in `dbg`, `fin`, and `opt` with both asset roots, 30 files and 1 segment each. I’m running the two focused debug executables once more for the detailed deformable test counts, then I’ll stage and commit.

Remote `main` is current relative to the local tracking ref; this checkout is only ahead. I’m staging the deformable source/test files explicitly and leaving `.cozter` unstaged.

The staged patch is 9 source/test files and passes the cached whitespace check. I’m committing it now as a focused deformable runtime hardening change.

Commit `8ed097c` is created. The only remaining uncommitted files are the local `.cozter` session/upload artifacts, so I’m pushing `main` to `origin/main` now.

Committed and pushed to `origin/main`.

Commit: `8ed097c` `Harden deformable hole edits`

I did one final standards pass before committing and fixed the EOF shape on two source files. Validation passed:

- `git diff --check`
- CRLF / final separator newline check for touched source files
- Builds: `dbg`, `fin`, `opt`
- `ctest -C dbg`, `ctest -C fin`, `ctest -C opt`: 7/7 passed in each config
- Focused debug tests: `assets_graphics_tests` 105 passed, `ecs_graphics_tests` 514 passed
- Isolated `resource_cooker` smokes: `dbg`, `fin`, `opt`, both asset roots, 30 files / 1 segment each

`main` is now synced with `origin/main`. The only remaining local uncommitted files are `.cozter/sessions/c93aeca0e5be.json` and `.cozter/uploads/`, which I left out per the repo rule for local session/upload artifacts.

summary:
Another document-led convergence cycle was run multiple times with no new defensible PR2-PR10 source fixes found; verification remained consistently green. After “proceed to next pr,” work shifted to PR11 (high-level surface edit/accessory attachment), with attempted edits across deformable API/test/testbed paths, but that effort became unstable: CRLF normalization and `apply_patch` churn caused dropped hunks, missing declarations in `impl/ecs_graphics/deformable_surface_edit.h` and `impl/ecs_graphics/ecs_graphics.h`, namespace mismatch in new tests (`NWB::Impl` vs `NWB::Core::ECSGraphics`), and compile failures. PR11 was not completed in this conversation.

On the explicit instruction to publish if no further fixes were found, the flow switched to final verification and release. EOF-shape drift was corrected in two source files; source/test changes were staged while `.cozter` artifacts were left unstaged. Verification passed: `git diff --check`; builds for `dbg/fin/opt`; `ctest` 7/7 in each config; focused debug tests (`assets_graphics_tests` 105, `ecs_graphics_tests` 514); isolated `resource_cooker` smokes for both `impl/assets` and `Testbed/assets` (30 files, 1 segment). Commit `8ed097c` (“Harden deformable hole edits”) was created and pushed to `origin/main`.
long_term: Application entrypoints must use `NWB_DEFINE_APPLICATION_ENTRY_POINT` in `global/application_entry.h`, and alloc/ECS executables and tests must link `nwb_common`, initialize `NWB::Core::Common::InitializerGuard`, and preserve dependency order with `nwb_common` before `nwb_alloc`.

Vulkan pipeline creation uses centralized descriptor-heap/layout helpers, owned layouts are destroyed in `core/graphics/vulkan/vulkan_resource_bindings.cpp`, and stencil-only formats must never use `VK_IMAGE_ASPECT_COLOR_BIT`.

Virtual-path override metadata validation belongs in assets via `Core::Assets::RejectVirtualPathOverrideField` in `core/assets/asset_paths.h`, and filesystem segment-transfer helpers remain split into typed read and typed write paths with `StagedDirectoryCleanupGuard` owning its log strings.

Non-`3rd_parties` code and assets must follow `.helper/standard.md` and `.gitattributes`, `global/detail/source_math/vector_stream/backend/scalar/scalar_transform_common.inl` intentionally has no `#pragma once`, C-runtime string comparisons use `NWB_STRCMP`, and `RuntimeException = std::runtime_error` remains in `global/type.h`.

The math layer uses column-vector semantics (`M * v`) with columns in `r[0..3]` and translation in `r[3]`, and double-precision parity coverage in `tests/math/math_tests.cpp` must stay synchronized including no-intrinsics targets.

Linux convergence verification is anchored at `__cmake/build/linux-clang-x64`, requires explicit multi-config `ctest -C <dbg|fin|opt>`, includes isolated `resource_cooker` smoke checks against both `impl/assets` and `Testbed/assets`, and repeats audit loops until no defensible source changes remain.

Local `.cozter/sessions/*.json` and `.cozter/uploads/*` are committed only on explicit request, `.cozter` keeps `compact_interval=10`, and explicit publish requests push directly to `main` after convergence checks.

ECS runtime uses shared `TransformComponent`, `CameraComponent`, `ProjectComponent`, and `LightComponent` with `ProjectComponent.mainCamera` as `EntityID`, plus project-first camera fallback and static-geometry transform gathering via `World::tryGetComponent`.

The deformable asset pipeline keeps separate typed asset/codec/cooker paths with required `asset.index_type`, streams `positions`/`normals`/`tangents`/`uv`/`indices`, allows optional `colors`/`source_samples`/`skin`/`morphs` to be explicitly empty, and rejects malformed limits/topology/consistency/frame data early.
Document-led deformable work is anchored to `.cozter/uploads/NWBLot_virtual_geometry_handoff.md`, with PR2-PR10 implemented and PR11 (high-level accessory/UI attachment flow) remaining future scope.

Runtime deformable editing keeps `sourceTriangleCount` stable, rejects malformed/degenerate/non-finite payloads plus malformed posed-hit frame data and negative picking `minDistance`, enforces strict PR9 hit identity and revision checks, and prefers `ScratchArena` for hot-path temporaries while deformer setup consumes payload pointer/count views.

Runtime triangle-provenance fallback is valid only on stable unedited revision-zero topology when `sourceTriangleCount` is known and runtime/source triangle counts match, and hole-edit provenance materialization must not synthesize a missing source triangle count.

Generated wall vertices must preserve recoverable canonical hit-based provenance (including explicit mixed provenance), and PR10 wall transfer uses exact rim copy plus local `0.25/0.5/0.25` inner inpainting for skin/morph/color with duplicate-joint merge and renormalization to `SkinInfluence4`.
Morph-weight accumulation and finite-value validation are centralized in `impl/ecs_graphics/deformable_runtime_helpers.h` and shared by `deformable_picking.cpp` and `deformer_system.cpp`.

`deformable_runtime_mesh_cache` treats `GpuUploadDirty` meshes as invalid for render and deformer consumption until uploads refresh GPU buffers, and deformable geometry binary decoding accepts only the current format version with a required displacement descriptor.
